how.title: How a 4-second check saves you €4,000.
how.lede: We don't show you opinions. We show you the same facts the RDW shows the police, plus an AI translation of what those facts mean for your wallet, your zone access, and your weekends. Three steps.

step1.title: Paste any Dutch plate
step1.body: Letters and numbers, with or without dashes. We accept whatever you copied from Marktplaats. No account needed.

step2.title: We pull the official record
step2.body: RDW registry, NAP odometer history, the full APK file, open recalls, fuel class. All from public sources, in about a second.

step3.title: Plain-language verdict
step3.body: Indicative price range, points of attention, monthly cost, emission-zone access in 2030. Read in 60 seconds, decide in 2 minutes.

redFlags.title: Eight things we surface that listings won't.
redFlags.subtitle: Each one of these has cost a buyer at least €1,000 we know about. They're the first thing we display on a dossier, not the last.
items: WOK status | RDW has refused to register this car. Walk away.
Mileage 'illogical' | NAP flags the odometer as tampered. Hard pass.
Open safety recall | Manufacturer issued a fix; this car never had it.
Short MOT, on purpose | A 1-month MOT after a long owner is the seller dodging a known bill.
Imported with no history | EU import on its second NL plate, no service stamps from origin.
Wrong emission class | Looks like Euro 6 in the ad, registered as Euro 5 at RDW. Zone trouble.
Hidden monthly cost | Heavy SUV in a high-opcenten province = €120+/month MRB alone.
Optimistic fuel claims | The RDW-registered consumption rarely matches the dealer 'fuel-efficient!' line.

about.title: An honest co-pilot for Dutch used-car buyers.
about.lede: Buying a used car in the Netherlands shouldn't require a glossary. WOK, BPM, milieuzone, Euro 6d-TEMP: none of that should stand between you and a fair decision. We do the translation.

mission.title: Why this exists
mission.body: Marktplaats and the big dealer portals optimise for click-throughs, not for buyers. The RDW already publishes the truth (it's public, it's free, it's structured) but it's spread across twelve pages and written for inspectors. We stitch it together, lay an AI translation on top, and put the things to worry about above the things to admire.

principles.title: What we stand for
items: Sourced facts only | Every line of data points back to RDW or a named third party. Our AI explains, never invents.
No recurring billing | Single Pass, Compare 3, Power 10: every pack is a one-time purchase. We never store your card.
Privacy by default | No tracking cookies, no resale of your searches. The first 5 lookups per day need no account at all.
Built for NL | Dutch BTW invoices, Dutch tax rules, Dutch milieuzone rules. We reply in Dutch when you email.

data.title: Where the data comes from
data.body: RDW Open Data covers registration, APK (MOT), fuel, CO₂, recalls and NAP odometer assessment. Autotelex Vehicle Data API supplies the indicative market range. AI summaries come from Anthropic Claude with RDW as the ground truth. We don't generate facts, we just translate them into plain language.

q.expiry: What happens when my pack expires?
q.data: Where does the data come from?

a.refund: Yes. Dutch consumer law gives you 14 days. Unused credits are refunded in full; we only charge for what you've used. Email support@autoconsul.nl with your order number.
a.expiry: Unused credits expire on the date shown on your dashboard. We send a reminder 3 days before. Buy a new pack any time and credits stack rather than replace.
a.tax: Yes. Every price is shown including 21% Dutch BTW. Your Stripe invoice has the BTW breakdown for your records or expense claim.
a.cancel: Nothing to cancel. Packs are one-off purchases. We never store your card and never charge again unless you actively buy another pack.
a.data: RDW Open Data for the free tier (registration, MOT, fuel, emissions, recalls). Autotelex for the paid market-value + dossier enrichment. AI summaries from Anthropic Claude with RDW as the ground truth.

glossary: RDW · Official|Data from the Dutch vehicle registry (RDW). Authoritative for legal status, APK, recalls.
AI · Indicative|Generated summary, not a guarantee. Lite is free when signed in; full analysis costs 1 credit.
APK / MOT|Periodic roadworthiness inspection. Short validity can mean the seller expects expensive repairs soon.
NAP|National odometer register assessment. "Logical" is good; "Illogical" is a serious red flag.
WOK|Official distrust marker. Do not buy a car with active WOK status.
Open recall|Manufacturer safety campaign not yet completed at the workshop.

kosten.title: Monthly running costs
kosten.badge: Indicative · Based on RDW + Dutch averages
kosten.subtitle: An indication of what this car costs you per month. MRB road tax is calculated exactly; fuel, insurance and maintenance are estimates. See each line for assumptions.
kosten.help: Estimate of the total monthly cost of owning this car. The road tax (MRB) is computed exactly from the official tables using weight, fuel type and your province. The other lines are realistic ranges; actual premiums and costs vary.
kosten.mrbHelp: Dutch motor-vehicle tax, paid quarterly to the Belastingdienst. Calculated from kerb weight × fuel type × provincial surcharge. Defaults to Noord-Holland (lowest surcharge at 67 %).
kosten.fuelHelp: Estimated fuel cost based on the official RDW combined consumption (WLTP cycle) over 15 000 km/year at current pump prices. ±15 % spread for driving style.
kosten.insuranceHelp: Indicative third-party + comprehensive cover for an average driver. Real premium depends on age, no-claims history, postcode and chosen cover. Quote with a broker for a binding figure.
kosten.maintenanceHelp: Service, oil, tyres and small repairs. Goes up with age, as older cars need more small fixes on average.
kosten.apkHelp: Monthly reserve for the annual / biennial MOT test plus the small repairs that typically come out of it.
kosten.disclaimer: All figures are indicative. For binding numbers: consult the Belastingdienst (road tax), an insurance broker (premium) and an independent garage (maintenance).

samples.value: Is this a fair asking price for this car?
samples.tax: How much Dutch road tax (MRB) will I pay?
samples.zones: Can I drive this into central Amsterdam?

chat.lockedBody: Chat about this car is part of every paid pack. Single Pass €4.95 unlocks 10 questions on this dossier. Ask about price, taxes, emission zones, anything.

help.brutoBpm: BPM is the Dutch one-off registration tax charged when a car is first registered. It scales with CO₂ emissions and fuel type. For used cars this is purely historical; the buyer doesn't pay it again.
help.nieuwprijs: The list price recorded by the RDW when the car was first registered. Not a current market value.
help.europeseVoertuigcategorie: EU vehicle category. M1 = passenger car (up to 8 seats), N1 = light commercial (≤ 3,500 kg), L = motorcycle/moped. Drives licence and road-use rules.
help.typegoedkeuringsnummer: The unique European type-approval number. Identifies the exact technical variant for regulators.
help.apkGeldigTot: APK is the Dutch periodic technical inspection (equivalent of the UK MOT). Cars: first APK after 4 years, then every 2 years, and yearly once the car is 8+ years old.
help.wamVerzekerd: Dutch mandatory third-party liability insurance (WAM). If 'No', the car cannot legally use public roads.
help.openstaandeTerugroepactie: A manufacturer recall that has not yet been performed on this car. Usually fixed free of charge by a brand dealer. Ask the service book or call the dealer.
help.wokStatus: Wacht Op Keuren: the RDW has flagged the vehicle (e.g. after damage or fraud suspicion) and requires re-inspection before it can be driven again.
help.tellerstandoordeel: The Dutch National Auto Pass odometer assessment. 'Logical' means registered odometer readings rise consistently. 'Illogical' means potential tampering, which is a serious red flag.
help.co2Uitstoot: CO₂ emissions in grams per kilometre under the WLTP cycle. Drives BPM tax and city emission-zone access.
help.zuinigheidslabel: Energy label A (most efficient) to G (least efficient), relative to the vehicle category. Sometimes affects employer-leasing perks.
help.uitlaatemissieniveau: Euro emission class (Euro 4, 5, 6 …). Determines whether the car may enter low-emission zones. Diesels below Euro 4 are often banned from Dutch city centres.
help.emissiecode: Sub-category within the Euro emission class. Used by zero-emission zones and exemptions.
help.hybrideKlasse: Mild hybrid (MHEV), full hybrid (HEV), plug-in hybrid (PHEV) or none. Affects tax incentives and charging needs.
help.massaLedig: Empty vehicle weight, excluding driver, fuel and luggage.
help.massaRijklaar: Ready-to-drive weight: empty vehicle + 90 % fuel + 75 kg driver. Used for Dutch road tax (MRB).
help.maxMassa: Permitted maximum total weight. Vehicle + cargo + occupants must not exceed this.
help.maxTrekkenGeremd: Maximum trailer / caravan weight when the trailer has its own brakes.
help.maxTrekkenOngeremd: Maximum trailer weight when the trailer has no brakes, usually noticeably lower.
help.vermogenMassa: Power per kilogram (kW/kg). A quick proxy for acceleration, useful for spotting sportier variants.
help.badgeRdw: Official data from the Dutch RDW vehicle registry. Refreshed daily by the RDW itself.
help.badgeAi: Interpretation by Auto Consul AI based on RDW data plus general market knowledge. Indicative, so verify before making binding decisions.
help.badgeMarktaanbod: Live listings from third-party marketplaces, pulled via Google. External pricing, not curated by us.

explainer.algemeen: The car's identity: make, model, colour, first-registration date and the original list price. This is what the RDW formally knows about the vehicle.
explainer.status: Today's legal and technical status. Is this car road-legal right now? Insured, with a valid MOT, and free of open recalls or warnings?
explainer.motorMilieu: Engine and emissions data. Fuel, power, consumption, CO₂ output and Euro emission class. Largely determines where the car may drive and what road tax it pays.
explainer.carrosserie: Dimensions and weights. Matters for road tax (kerb weight), maximum tow weight for caravans/trailers, and garage or parking constraints.
explainer.apkHistorie: Every APK inspection on file, with date, type and any points raised. Repeated defects over time often tell a story.

footer.about: Auto Consul combines the official RDW Open Data with AI-driven explanations so you know exactly what you're getting into before you buy a car. Independent and free for anonymous searches.
